name_en: Stone Stupa Group at Nyui Kodō
description_en: A group of stone stupa (itabi) tablets classified as archaeological material within the tangible cultural properties category. It is municipally designated by the city of Hirosaki, located at Nyui-Kodō, Hirosaki. The designation was made on 1998-11-27.

name_en: Suzu Ware Sutra Container and Lid Stone
description_en: A Suzu ware sutra container and its stone lid, classified as archaeological material within the tangible cultural properties category. It is municipally designated by the city of Hirosaki and held at the Hirosaki City Museum in Shimoshirogane-cho. The designation was made on 2016-11-04.

name_en: Clay Figurine (Excavated from the Sunazawa Site)
description_en: A clay figurine (dogū) excavated from the Sunazawa site, classified as archaeological material within the tangible cultural properties category. It is municipally designated by the city of Hirosaki and held at the Hirosaki City Museum in Shimoshirogane-cho. The designation was made on 2022-10-13.

name_en: Large Jade Bead (Excavated from the Yukuchi Nagane Site)
description_en: A large jade bead excavated from the Yukuchi Nagane site, classified as archaeological material within the tangible cultural properties category. It is municipally designated by the city of Hirosaki and held at the buried cultural property storage facility in Jumoku 3-chome. The designation was made on 2022-10-13.

A stone stupa tablet bearing pictorial imagery, classified as historical material within the tangible cultural properties category. It is municipally designated by the city of Hirosaki, located in Shinmachi, Hirosaki. The designation was made on 1982-01-27.

name_en: Sword with Vermilion Signature Enju Kunitoki
description_en: A sword bearing the vermilion signature Enju Kunitoki, classified as historical material within the tangible cultural properties category. It is municipally designated by the city of Hirosaki and held at the Hirosaki City Museum in Shimoshirogane-cho. The designation was made on 1985-01-22.

name_romaji: Gashi Kuyō Myōgōtō・Gashi Kuyō Daimokutō (Gashi Kuyō Myōgōtō)
name_en: Famine Memorial Nembutsu Pillar (part of a famine memorial pillar pair)
description_en: A stone memorial pillar for famine victims inscribed with a Buddhist invocation, classified as historical material within the tangible cultural properties category. It is municipally designated by the city of Hirosaki, located in Higashiwatoku-cho, Hirosaki. The designation was made on 1990-10-20.

name_romaji: Gashi Kuyō Myōgōtō・Gashi Kuyō Daimokutō (Gashi Kuyō Daimokutō)
name_en: Famine Memorial Daimoku Pillar (part of a famine memorial pillar pair)
description_en: A stone memorial pillar for famine victims inscribed with a Nichiren daimoku invocation, classified as historical material within the tangible cultural properties category. It is municipally designated by the city of Hirosaki, located in Higashiwatoku-cho, Hirosaki. The designation was made on 1990-10-20.

name_en: Kōshin Stone Pillar with Shōmen Kongō Image
description_en: A Kōshin stone pillar bearing an image of the deity Shōmen Kongō, classified as historical material within the tangible cultural properties category. It is municipally designated by the city of Hirosaki, located at Akudo, Hirosaki. The designation was made on 1993-04-26.

name_en: Kōshin Stone Pillar with the Kōmyō Shingon Mantra
description_en: A Kōshin stone pillar inscribed with the Kōmyō Shingon mantra, classified as historical material within the tangible cultural properties category. It is municipally designated by the city of Hirosaki, located at Sakamoto, Hirosaki. The designation was made on 1993-04-26.

Military battle standards classified as historical material within the tangible cultural properties category. It is municipally designated by the city of Hirosaki and held at the Takaoka no Mori Hirosaki-han Museum of History in Takaoka. The designation was made on 1992-01-31.

A collection of materials dedicated by Kida Inaki, classified as historical material within the tangible cultural properties category. It is municipally designated by the city of Hirosaki and held at the Takaoka no Mori Hirosaki-han Museum of History in Takaoka. The designation was made on 1992-01-31.

The Bokuden-ryū school of swordsmanship, an intangible cultural property in the classical martial arts subcategory. It is municipally designated by the city of Hirosaki. The designation was made on 2009-04-15.

The Tōda-ryū school of swordsmanship, an intangible cultural property in the classical martial arts subcategory. It is municipally designated by the city of Hirosaki. The designation was made on 2019-11-01.

name_romaji: Shihon Tansai Shiki Nōkō no Zu Hirao Rosen Hitsu Rokkyoku Byōbu
name_en: Six-Panel Folding Screen of the Four Seasons of Farming, Painted by Hirao Rosen (light color on paper)
description_en: A six-panel folding screen depicting the four seasons of farming, in light color on paper and painted by Hirao Rosen, classified as a tangible folk cultural property. It is municipally designated by the city of Hirosaki and held at the Hirosaki City Museum in Shimoshirogane-cho. The designation was made on 1966-01-04.

A stone statue of Shō Kannon (the Sacred Avalokiteshvara), classified as a tangible folk cultural property. It is municipally designated by the city of Hirosaki, located in Shinmachi, Hirosaki. The designation was made on 1982-01-27.
The Ishikawa lion dance, an intangible folk cultural property. It is municipally designated by the city of Hirosaki. The designation was made on 1965-09-14.
The Onizawa lion/deer dance (shishiodori), an intangible folk cultural property. It is municipally designated by the city of Hirosaki. The designation was made on 1965-09-14.
The Tsugaru lion dance of Matsumori-cho, an intangible folk cultural property. It is municipally designated by the city of Hirosaki. The designation was made on 1982-07-15.
The Godai lion dance, an intangible folk cultural property. It is municipally designated by the city of Hirosaki. The designation was made on 1983-09-28.

The Kuniyoshi lion/deer dance (shishiodori), an intangible folk cultural property. It is municipally designated by the city of Hirosaki, located at Kuniyoshi, Hirosaki. The designation was made on 1985-08-24.
The Toriino lion/deer dance (shishiodori), an intangible folk cultural property. It is municipally designated by the city of Hirosaki. The designation was made on 1986-01-08.
The Onizawa naked pilgrimage (hadaka mairi), an intangible folk cultural property. It is municipally designated by the city of Hirosaki. The designation was made on 2002-03-29.
The Kamisukisawa lion dance, an intangible folk cultural property. It is municipally designated by the city of Hirosaki. The designation was made on 2015-12-24.

name_romaji: Yoshida Shōin Raiyū no Chi Tsuketari Shōinshitsu
name_en: Site of Yoshida Shōin's Visit, with the Shōin Room
description_en: A historic site marking the visit of Yoshida Shōin, together with the associated Shōin Room, in the historic site subcategory of the monuments category. It is municipally designated by the city of Hirosaki, located in Motonaga-cho, Hirosaki. The designation was made on 1978-02-01.

name_en: Kakushū-ji Temple of the Sōtō Zen Sect
description_en: Kakushū-ji, a temple of the Sōtō Zen sect (mountain name Tsugaru-san), in the historic site subcategory of the monuments category. It is municipally designated by the city of Hirosaki, located in Fujishiro 1-chome, Hirosaki. The designation was made on 1985-01-24.

The Dōgataira sutra mound, in the historic site subcategory of the monuments category. It is municipally designated by the city of Hirosaki, located at Ōsawa, Dōgataira, Hirosaki. The designation was made on 2016-11-04.

A natural monument known as the Five Cedars, in the natural monument subcategory of the monuments category. It is municipally designated by the city of Hirosaki, located at Miyazawa, Hirosaki. The designation was made on 1998-11-05.

A weeping cherry tree (shidarezakura) designated as a natural monument in the monuments category. It is municipally designated by the city of Hirosaki, located at Takaoka, Hirosaki. The designation was made on 1998-11-05.

A Nikko fir tree (urajiromomi) designated as a natural monument in the monuments category. It is municipally designated by the city of Hirosaki, located at Takaoka, Hirosaki. The designation was made on 1998-11-05.

A sawara cypress designated as a natural monument in the monuments category. It is municipally designated by the city of Hirosaki, located at Takaoka, Hirosaki. The designation was made on 1998-11-05.

A rock-splitting oak and a harigiri (castor aralia) tree, designated as a natural monument in the monuments category. It is municipally designated by the city of Hirosaki, located at Hachiman, Hirosaki. The designation was made on 1998-11-05.

A kobushi magnolia tree designated as a natural monument in the monuments category. It is municipally designated by the city of Hirosaki, located at Shinboshi, Hirosaki. The designation was made on 1998-11-05.

name_en: Materials Related to the Kitabatake Family
description_en: A collection of materials related to the Kitabatake family, including the family's former residence, a brocade banner, a hitatare sleeve, the Eiroku diary, a framed sketch map of the Furudate castle site, and old documents, classified within the tangible cultural properties category. It is town-designated by Itayanagi. The designation was made on 1984-10-26.

The main gate (sanmon) of Shōkyū-ji temple (mountain name Goun-zan), a structure within the tangible cultural properties category. It is town-designated by Itayanagi. The designation was made on 1986-03-20.

name_en: Front Entrance of the Former Town Hall Building
description_en: The front entrance of the former town hall building, a structure within the tangible cultural properties category. It is town-designated by Itayanagi. The designation was made on 1986-03-20.
The Seven Lucky Gods dance of Omotemachi, a folk performing art within the folk cultural properties category. It is town-designated by Itayanagi. The designation was made on 1987-05-22.
The Gorindaira sword-swinging performance (tachifuri), a folk performing art within the folk cultural properties category. It is town-designated by Itayanagi. The designation was made on 1987-05-22.
A sawara cypress and a Japanese yew (ichii), designated as a natural monument in the monuments category. It is town-designated by Itayanagi. The designation was made on 1993-02-19.
A large ceremonial banner (ōnobori) of Hōryō Daigongen, classified within the tangible cultural properties category. It is town-designated by Itayanagi. The designation was made on 2003-02-26.
